page: Microsoft Azure D48_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv4 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture. The configuration features 48 dedicated virtual CPUs, derived from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. It includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard compute and memory operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU evaluations place this server in the middle 50% tier, with scores of 1405.08 and 44706.09 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show strong results in the top 25% tier for large-block uncached read and write operations, reaching 189.04 GB/sec and 92.68 GB/sec. However, workload-specific tests reveal limitations; while prompt processing for small and medium language models falls into the average category, text generation speeds are poor, placing in the bottom 10% tier.

The D48_v4 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads, such as static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks. The lack of local storage requires reliance on external storage volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and strong large-block memory bandwidth provide reliable throughput for standard business applications without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. It is best suited for application servers, relational databases, and general-purpose containerized environments.
description: Microsoft Azure D48_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it provides 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores and 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for most compute tasks, though it demonstrates strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth. Conversely, text generation for language models is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10%. The D48_v4 is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard enterprise workloads requiring steady CPU and memory resources, such as web serving, database management, and general-purpose application hosting, provided external storage is configured.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced compute and strong memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and strong memory bandwidth for standard enterprise workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
